syllabus.topic: Course Context & Pattern Representation Learning Overview
sub_points: Positioning of the lecture within Pattern Recognition (EE5907) and representation learning
Learning goals and roadmap: unsupervised soft clustering vs. supervised ensemble learning
High-level motivation for probabilistic models and classifier combination methods

syllabus.topic: Soft Clustering with Mixture Models and Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM)
sub_points: Soft clustering concept: probabilistic (fractional) assignment of points to clusters
Real-world motivation/use case: customer segmentation as clustering without labels
Mixture model formulation as weighted sums of component densities
GMM definition and interpretation (mixture weights, means, covariances)
Problem setup: fitting K Gaussians to unlabeled data (density estimation / clustering)

syllabus.topic: GMM Maximum Likelihood and the EM Algorithm (Derivation & Updates)
sub_points: Log-likelihood formulation for GMM parameters and latent assignment variables
E-step: computing responsibilities (posterior membership probabilities)
M-step: re-estimating mixture weights, component means, and covariances via partial derivatives/closed-form updates
Initialization strategies and convergence behavior (illustrated through iterative parameter/weight updates)
Practical considerations: monotonic likelihood increase and stopping criteria

syllabus.topic: Limitations and Practical Issues of GMM
sub_points: Model assumptions and failure modes (e.g., Gaussian shape constraints, sensitivity to covariance structure)
Sensitivity to initialization and local optima in EM
Choosing number of components K and risk of overfitting/underfitting
Potential numerical issues (e.g., singular covariances) and mitigation ideas

syllabus.topic: Boosting (Supervised Learning) and Weak-to-Strong Classifier Construction
sub_points: Core idea of boosting: combining multiple weak learners to form a strong classifier
Sequential training process: iteratively focusing on previously misclassified samples
Weak learner family example (linear/threshold decision rules) and additive model view
Updating sample weights / reweighting errors and interpreting misclassification emphasis
Connection between boosting variants and choices of cost functions/optimization procedures

syllabus.topic: AdaBoost Algorithm: Formulation and Training Details
sub_points: AdaBoost setup for binary classification with labels in {−1, +1}
Definition of weak learner outputs, weighted error, and learner coefficient (alpha)
Sample weight update rule and normalization across rounds
Final strong classifier as a weighted vote (sign of weighted sum)
Interpretation as minimizing an exponential loss / margin improvement

syllabus.topic: Boosting for Object Detection: Haar Features, Integral Images, and Strong Classifiers
sub_points: Object detection pipeline using boosting: scanning windows/patches and classification
Haar-like filters for feature extraction and efficient computation via integral images
Transforming continuous features into thresholded weak classifiers (decision stumps)
Building a strong classifier by selecting and weighting discriminative features across rounds
Illustrative examples of feature selection and aggregation into a detector

syllabus.topic: Detection Challenges, Hard Negatives, and Facial Feature Localization
sub_points: Motivation for handling difficult negative/background samples (hard negatives) in training
Implications of fixed-position feature extraction (alignment/pose sensitivity)
Application examples: face/profile detection and facial feature localization
Qualitative evaluation: locating the target (head) and failure cases
Reference/reading pointer (Friedman, Hastie, Tibshirani) connecting boosting to broader statistical learning theory
